headline: Richtech Robotics Lawsuits Put Microsoft Ties And Growth Outlook In Focus
summary: Richtech Robotics (NasdaqCM:RR) is facing multiple securities class action lawsuits over allegations that it misrepresented the nature of its relationship with Microsoft. Microsoft has reportedly said the engagement is limited to a standard customer program, not a broader commercial partnership. Law firms are seeking shareholders to act as lead plaintiffs, with regulatory attention increasing as key filing deadlines approach. For investors watching Richtech Robotics at a share price of...

headline: New York Pension Wants Data Centers to Be Good Neighbors
summary: Pension funds are joining the crusade to shield communities from the noisy, energy-guzzling impact of AI data centers.  The New York State Common Retirement Fund wrote to Amazon, Meta Platforms, Alphabet and 15 other tech companies, asking them to prioritize the needs of communities where they own, operate, or lease data centers.  In a letter, it said this was to “protect shareholder value and ensure sustainable growth.”

content.title: New York Pension Wants Data Centers to Be Good Neighbors
content.summary: Pension funds are joining the crusade to shield communities from the noisy, energy-guzzling impact of AI data centers.  The New York State Common Retirement Fund wrote to Amazon, Meta Platforms, Alphabet and 15 other tech companies, asking them to prioritize the needs of communities where they own, operate, or lease data centers.  In a letter, it said this was to “protect shareholder value and ensure sustainable growth.”

thumbnail.caption: Pivot Point Consulting is a KLAS-ranked consulting leader dedicated to empowering healthcare organizations to maximize the value derived from their investments. Backed by Vaco Holdings, Pivot Point Consulting’s comprehensive suite of services spans managed services, analytics and AI, EHR, ERP, advisory, virtual care and cybersecurity. They deliver top-tier consulting, managed services and talent solutions to providers, payers, life sciences and technology organizations. (PRNewsfoto/Pivot Point Consulting)

headline: Microsoft Enterprise Agreement Pricing Increases and Discount Tier Collapse Raise 2026 Renewal Risk, Report From Info-Tech Research Group
summary: Microsoft Enterprise Agreement renewals are entering a materially different pricing environment following the elimination of tiered discount structures and a series of announced increases across core Microsoft 365 and server licenses. Effective November 1, 2025, Enterprise Agreement discount tiers were collapsed, resetting pricing dynamics for many organizations. Additional SKU-level increases and upcoming July 1, 2026, adjustments further shift the financial calculus for IT and procurement lead

content.title: Microsoft Enterprise Agreement Pricing Increases and Discount Tier Collapse Raise 2026 Renewal Risk, Report From Info-Tech Research Group
content.summary: Microsoft Enterprise Agreement renewals are entering a materially different pricing environment following the elimination of tiered discount structures and a series of announced increases across core Microsoft 365 and server licenses. Effective November 1, 2025, Enterprise Agreement discount tiers were collapsed, resetting pricing dynamics for many organizations. Additional SKU-level increases and upcoming July 1, 2026, adjustments further shift the financial calculus for IT and procurement lead

thumbnail.caption: The Microsoft Year-End 2026 report from Info-Tech Research Group provides IT and procurement leaders with early guidance to assess impact and enter negotiations with independent pricing validation and a structured renewal strategy. (CNW Group/Info-Tech Research Group)
